contract.name: Pressure Russia North
contract.description: Push into Russia’s northern holdings by coordinating pressure on St. Petersburg and nearby seas, often with northern allies while Austria advances elsewhere. Use it when Russia is overextended or tied

contract.name: Fortify the Frontier
contract.description: Use this build when Austria has an open home center after the winter adjustments to strengthen its borders. Add an army in Vienna or Trieste, or a fleet in Trieste, to cover key fronts against Italy,

contract.name: Secure Defensive Line
contract.description: Form a tight Austrian defensive shell—typically holding Vienna/Serbia while fleets anchor Greece/Trieste—to cover home centers and key approaches. Use it when pressured by Italy, Turkey, or Russia, es

contract.name: Secure Balkan Frontier
contract.description: As Austria, secure the Balkans by taking and holding Serbia, Greece, and Albania while keeping Vienna covered and the Adriatic under control. Use this in 1901–1902 to stabilize your southern frontier

contract.name: Open Serbia Galicia
contract.description: Austria opens with A Bud→Ser and A Vie→Gal, usually alongside F Tri→Alb or hold, to claim Serbia while contesting Galicia immediately. Use it when you want early pressure on Russia and a strong Balkan
